evidences.title: Extract Historical Data from Binance Using Python | Medium
evidences.content: from binance.client import Clientimport pandas as pdimport datetime# Binance API credentialsapi_key = 'your_api_key'api_secret = 'your_api_secret'client = Client(api_key, api_secret)# Define the symbol for BTC/USDT pairsymbol = 'BTCUSDT'# Define custom start and end timestart_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 3, 15, 0, 0, 0)end_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 15, 0, 0, 0)klines = client.get_historical_klines(symbol=symbol, interval=Client.KLINE_INTERVAL_1MINUTE, start_str=str(start_time), end_str=str(end_time))# Convert the data into a pandas dataframe for easier manipulationdf_M = pd.DataFrame(klines, columns=['Open Time', 'Open', 'High', 'Low', 'Close', 'Volume', 'Close Time', 'Quote Asset Volume', 'Number of Trades', 'Taker Buy Base Asset Volume', 'Taker Buy Quote Asset Volume', [...] ``` symbol = 'BTCUSDT' 'BTCUSDT' ``` We can set a custom start and end time for our function by parsing the date to date time format. ``` # format (year, month, day, hour, minute, second)start_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 3, 15, 0, 0, 0)end_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 15, 0, 0, 0) # format (year, month, day, hour, minute, second) 2024 3 15 0 0 0 2024 6 15 0 0 0 ``` `datetime.datetime`: Cr...

evidences.title: Get historical klines from binance api - GitHub
evidences.content: #### CLI Options | Option | Alias | Description | --- | `--pair` | `-p` | Trading pair (e.g., BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT) | | `--interval` | `-i` | Kline interval (1m, 3m, 5m, 15m, 30m, 1h, 2h, 4h, 6h, 8h, 12h, 1d, 3d, 1w) | | `--start` | `-s` | Start date (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO 8601) | | `--end` | `-e` | End date (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO 8601) | | `--output` | `-o` | Output directory path (filename is auto-generated) | | `--format` | `-f` | Output format (json or csv, default: json) | | `--help` | `-h` | Display help | | `--version` | `-V` | Display version | #### Hybrid mode You can also provide some options and be prompted for the rest: ``` # You will be prompted for start date, end date, and output path # ``` ### Library [...] ## Installation ### Via Homebrew (macOS/Linux) ``` brew install maxgfr/tap/binance-historical ``` ### Via npm ``` npm install -g binance-historical ``` ### Download binary Download the latest binary for your platform from the releases page. ## Usage ### CLI #### Interactive mode ``` binance-historical download ``` #### Non-interactive mode Pass all options as arguments: ``` binance-historical download \ --pair BTCUSDT \ --interval 4h \ --start 2024-01-01 \ --end 2024-12-3...

evidences.title: ET to UTC Converter - Time.now
evidences.content: Time Difference: UTC is 5 hours ahead of EST (winter) and 4 hours ahead of EDT (summer). The difference changes twice yearly with daylight saving transitions. #### Daylight Saving Time Impact Winter Period (EST): From first Sunday in November to second Sunday in March, ET is UTC-5, making UTC 5 hours ahead Summer Period (EDT): From second Sunday in March to first Sunday in November, ET is UTC-4, making UTC 4 hours ahead UTC Stability: UTC never changes and remains constant year-round, making it ideal for international coordination and technical applications #### Common Conversion Examples Business Hours (Winter) EST 9:00 AM → UTC 2:00 PM EST 5:00 PM → UTC 10:00 PM 5-hour difference during winter Business Hours (Summer) EDT 9:00 AM → UTC 1:00 PM EDT 5:00 PM → UTC 9:00 PM [...] EDT 9:00 AM → UTC 1:00 PM EDT 5:00 PM → UTC 9:00 PM 4-hour difference during summer International Coordination EST Midnight → UTC 5:00 AM EDT Midnight → UTC 4:00 AM UTC used for global scheduling Technical Applications Server logs → UTC timestamps API calls → UTC standards UTC preferred for technical systems #### Conversion Tips & Best Practices • Add 5 hours to EST (winter) or 4 hours to EDT (summer) to ge...

evidences.title: Binance.US API Documentation: Introduction
evidences.content: Data Source: Memory ### Get Candlestick Data > Example ``` -X "GET"" ``` ``` import requests resp = requests. get(' print(resp. json()) ``` > Response ``` ``` `GET /api/v3/klines` Use this endpoint to get Kline/candlestick bars for a token symbol. Klines are uniquely identified by their open time. Please note the maximum limit is 1,000 bars. Weight: 1 Parameters: | Name | Type | Mandatory | Description | --- --- | | symbol | STRING | YES | | interval | ENUM | YES | | startTime | LONG | NO | | endTime | LONG | NO | | limit | INT | NO | Default 500; max 1000 | If startTime and endTime are not sent, the most recent klines are returned. Data Source: Database ## Price Data ### Get Live Ticker Price > Example [...] secret_key = "NhqPtmdSJYdKjVHjA7PZj4Mge3R5YNiP1e3UZjInClVN65XAbvqqM6A7H5fATj0j" uri_path ="/api/v3/openOrders" data ={"symbol": "BTCUSDT", "timestamp": 1499827319559} get_open_order_result = binanceus_request(uri_path, data, api_key, secret_key) [...] `OCO` Order types (orderTypes, type): `LIMIT` `MARKET` `STOP_LOSS_LIMIT` `TAKE_PROFIT_LIMIT` `LIMIT_MAKER` Order side (side): `BUY` `SELL` Time in force (timeInForce): | Status | Description | --- | | `GTC` | "Good Till Cancel...

evidences.title: Market Data Only | Binance Open Platform
evidences.content: Skip to main content # Market Data Only URLs These URLs do not require any authentication (i.e. The API key is not necessary) and serve only public market data. ### RESTful API​ On the RESTful API, these are the endpoints you can request on `data-api.binance.vision`: GET /api/v3/aggTrades GET /api/v3/avgPrice GET /api/v3/depth GET /api/v3/exchangeInfo GET /api/v3/klines GET /api/v3/ping GET /api/v3/ticker GET /api/v3/ticker/24hr GET /api/v3/ticker/bookTicker GET /api/v3/ticker/price GET /api/v3/time GET /api/v3/trades GET /api/v3/uiKlines Sample request: ``` curl -sX GET " curl -sX GET " ``` ### Websocket Streams​ [...] ### Websocket Streams​ Public market data can also be retrieved through the websocket market data using the URL `data-stream.binance.vision`. The streams available through this domain are the same that can be found in the Websocket Market Streams documentation. Note that User Data Streams cannot be accessed through this URL. Sample request: ``` wss://data-stream.binance.vision:443/ws/btcusdt@kline_1mwss://data-stream.binance.vision:443/ws/btcusdt@kline_1m ``` RESTful API Websocket Streams

evidences.title: EST to UTC
evidences.content: Based International Air Transport Association (IATA) timezone codes. Time zones use hours and minutes offset from Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), most a whole number of hours, a few are offset by 30 or 45 minutes. Eastern Standard Time (EST) is UTC-5:00, and Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) is UTC-4:00, this time zone is called Eastern Time Zone (ET) in the United States, parts of eastern Canada, Mexico, Panama and the Caribbean Islands. Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) is the primary time standard now, time zones around the world are expressed using offsets from UTC, UTC offset is the difference in hours and minutes from UTC, a time zone can be determined by adding or subtracting the number of UTC offset. evidences.content: ### What are the national holidays in the EST time zone? National holidays: New Year's Day: Celebrated on January 1st, marking the start of the new year. Independence Day: Observed on July 4th, with parades, fireworks, and celebrations throughout the region. Labor Day: Celebrated on the first Monday in September, honoring American workers and their contributions. Thanksgiving Day: Observed on the fourth Thursday in November, a major holiday for family gatherings and feasting. Christmas Day: Celebrated on December 25th, marked by family gatherings, feasts, and festive celebrations. ### What is some local trivia or geography about the EST region? [...] ### What is the difference between EST and EDT? EDT (Eastern Daylight Time) is used during Daylight Saving time and is UTC-4 hours. EST (Eastern Standard Time) is used outside of Daylight Saving Time and is UTC-5 hours. ### What are some major industries in the EST time zone? [...] ### What are some major industries in the EST time zone? Major industries: Finance: New York City is a global financial hub, home to the New York Stock Exchange and Wall Street. Healthcare: The healthcare and pharmaceutical industries are significant, wit...
